things.name: Casino de Monte-Carlo
things.description: The world's most glamorous casino opened in 1863, with ornate Belle Epoque interiors and the legendary square made famous by James Bond

things.name: Prince's Palace of Monaco
things.description: Official residence of the Grimaldi family since 1297, with state apartments open to visitors and the daily Changing of the Guard ceremony

things.name: Monaco Oceanographic Museum
things.description: Cliff-top museum founded by Prince Albert I in 1910 with world-class aquariums, shark lagoon and marine science collections

things.name: Monaco Grand Prix Circuit
things.description: The world's most famous Formula 1 street circuit weaving through Monaco's tight roads; walk the hairpin bends and tunnel year-round

things.name: Jardin Exotique
things.description: Spectacular clifftop garden with the world's finest collection of succulent plants and cacti, with panoramic views over Monaco and the sea

things.name: Monaco Cathedral
things.description: Romanesque-Byzantine cathedral of 1875 built with white La Turbie stone; burial place of Princess Grace Kelly and the Grimaldi princes

things.name: Stade Louis II
things.description: Home of AS Monaco FC with a 18,500 capacity; Monaco's modern stadium built partly over the sea on a bold cantilevered platform

things.name: Opera de Monte-Carlo
things.description: Stunning Garnier opera house attached to the casino, designed by Charles Garnier in 1879 with lavish gold and red velvet interiors

things.name: Port Hercule
things.description: Monaco's glamorous harbour crammed with superyachts and cruise ships; the Formula 1 start-finish straight runs along its quayside

things.name: Palais Princier Gardens
things.description: Formal French-style gardens adjacent to the palace with fragrant orange trees, rose beds and views over the Mediterranean and Cap d'Ail
